title: Imaging the Magnetically Driven Reconstruction of the Electronic States in the Antiferromagnetic Topological Insulator EuSn$_2$As$_2$
authors: Luka Khizanishvili, Erekle Jmukhadze, Anika Raisa, Divyanshi Sar, Mingda Gong, Tetiana Romanova, Dariusz Kaczorowski, Wei-Cheng Lee, Pegor Aynajian
abstract: The realization of the axion insulator phase in magnetic topological insulators is often hindered by crystalline symmetries that protect gapless surface states, even when time-reversal symmetry is broken. Here, we use variable-temperature scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) and spectroscopy (STS), complemented with density functional theory (DFT), to investigate the local electronic structure of the antiferromagnetic (AFM) topological insulator EuSn$_2$As$_2$ across its Néel transition at $T_N = 24$ K. On the (001) surface, we observe a substantial density of intrinsic Sn vacancies that introduce nanoscale electronic inhomogeneity and p-type doping. Upon cooling below $T_N$, we resolve the emergence of two distinct magnetically driven gaps: a $\sim$100 meV gap near the Fermi level and a $\sim$50 meV gap at the ARPES-resolved Dirac point. We attribute the former gap to AFM Brillouin-zone folding and hybridization. The characteristics of the 50 meV gap point toward the lifting of mirror-symmetry protection by Sn vacancies and the consequent mass gapping of the Dirac point, although contributions from AFM-induced folding hybridization cannot be entirely ruled out. Our findings provide real-space evidence for strong coupling between localized moments and itinerant topological states, highlighting exfoliable EuSn$_2$As$_2$ as a potential candidate for realizing axion-insulator-based devices.
summary: This work uses STM and DFT to probe the electronic structure of the magnetic topological insulator EuSn2As2 across its magnetic transition. The key finding is the appearance of two distinct energy gaps below the Néel temperature, which the authors attribute to magnetic ordering and symmetry breaking. This highlights the material's potential for realizing axion-insulator-based devices.
main_problem: The study investigates the magnetically driven reconstruction of electronic states in the antiferromagnetic topological insulator EuSn2As2 across its Néel transition.
main_result: The research observes two distinct magnetically driven gaps (100 meV and 50 meV) opening below the Néel temperature, providing real-space evidence of strong coupling between localized magnetic moments and itinerant topological states.
method: The study combines variable-temperature Scanning Tunneling Microscopy/Spectroscopy (STM/STS) with Density Functional Theory (DFT) calculations.
model_or_system: The system is the antiferromagnetic topological insulator EuSn2As2, which is studied in both paramagnetic and antiferromagnetic phases. The electronic structure evolution is mapped across the magnetic transition temperature (T_N = 24 K).
key_observables: Emergence of two distinct energy gaps (100 meV near E_F and 50 meV at the Dirac point), electronic inhomogeneity due to Sn vacancies, and temperature dependence of the gaps.
important_parameters: Néel temperature (T_N = 24 K), gap energies (100 meV, 50 meV), and the magnetic ordering parameter.
main_assumptions: The 50 meV gap opening is attributed to the lifting of mirror-symmetry protection by Sn vacancies, although contributions from AFM-induced folding hybridization cannot be entirely ruled out.
figures_summary: Figures show crystal structures, DFT calculations for PM and AFM phases, and STM/STS spectra/maps illustrating the opening of the 100 meV gap and the 50 meV gap at different energies and defect locations.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing the physical system and methods (STM/STS, DFT), then detailing the observation of surface defects and the two distinct temperature-dependent gaps, and finally attributing the origin of these gaps to specific symmetry breakings (magnetic/structural).
why_interesting: The strong coupling between localized magnetic moments (Eu 4f) and itinerant topological states is a key area of modern condensed matter physics, relevant to spintronics and topological quantum materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
